item.title: How Biladi and Blue Shield are helping to preserve the cultural heritage of war-torn countries
item.description: Non-governmental organisations Biladi and Blue Shield are among the design experts using new technologies to create detailed records of cultural heritage and help rebuild after conflict.

item.title: 'I wasn't expecting the drama': What you've learnt since owning a pet
item.description: Pets can be lifelong companions, but they can also test us in ways we never imagined. We asked you to share your stories. Here's what you told us.

item.title: Wet conditions set to ease in parts of Queensland after spell of 'unseasonal' rain
item.description: Most Queenslanders are in for warmer and brighter weather this weekend, but scattered showers are expected to continue along parts of the coast.

item.title: While Britain's defence strategy comes under fire, the nuclear arms race continues
item.description: The prospect of nuclear weapons being used in either Ukraine or the Middle East, rather than being fired between Moscow, Washington or Beijing has risen.

item.title: Abbott and Hanson's ancient feud is rife with political drama and biblical comeuppance
item.description: Tony Abbott has been back in the political cycle for barely a fortnight and already the air is redolent with garment-rending, ancient feuds, biblical comeuppance and a listening tour.

item.title: How university degrees became a dating deal breaker
item.description: Australia's so-called power couples, in which both partners are university-educated, have almost quadrupled over 20 years. Uni degrees could be forging a dating divide, with profound implications for social inequality.

item.title: Parts of Australia on track for warmest start to winter on record amid influx of tropical air
item.description: An influx of humid, tropical air has resulted in a remarkably warm and relatively wet start to winter across Australia.

item.title: Scientists share discoveries about Australia's polar past and the dinosaurs that roamed
item.description: Palaeontologists say evidence pointing to Australia's polar past shows that many dinosaurs roamed the country when the south east was still within the Antarctic Circle.

item.title: Just how far can the Socceroos go at the FIFA World Cup, and what is a pass mark?
item.description: This crop of Socceroos has been labelled the "next generation", but that doesn't mean they aren't aiming high at the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Where are the 2026 World Cup stadiums and which games are they hosting?
item.description: From volcano mimics to modern super-stadia with retractable roofs, the grounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup are among the most spectacular on Earth. Here's your guide to each of the 16 stadiums across Canada, Mexico and the United States.

item.title: Agnes Water films 'time capsule' documentary to record life in 2026
item.description: Schoolchildren starring in a documentary about their quaint home town on the Queensland coast hope the publicity does not change what they love about it.

item.title: Australia's new ambassador gets to work in Washington ahead of 'robust' tariff negotiation
item.description: Greg Moriarty says he will "robustly" push back on the Trump administration's "extremely disappointing" new tariff plan and may recruit Australian businesses to help take up the fight.

item.title: Why we cannot name high-profile Queensland man at centre of Cairns extortion case
item.description: There is a highly secretive and unusual court case playing out behind closed doors in Far North Queensland involving a prominent married man and his alleged affair, a non-publication order we are not allowed to see and an allegation of extortion we cannot tell you much about.

item.title: The Interview: Reuben Kaye on the cost and the nourishment of being an artist in Australia
item.description: The new artistic director of the Adelaide Cabaret Festival shares what's unique about the art form in Australia and what his teenage self would think about who he is today.

item.title: Feral sambar deer likely brought to SA for hunting purposes, Invasive Species Council says
item.description: One of Australia's largest feral deer species was spotted in SA's Riverland for the first time earlier this month. While its existence is being investigated, the Invasive Species Council says it was likely transported across state lines for hunting purposes.

item.title: How 'Eternal Darkness', Israel's operation in southern Lebanon, continues to expand
item.description: The so-called ceasefire since April, which Hezbollah is not a party to, has done little to halt the death and destruction in southern Lebanon. Here's how the scale of the Israeli operation called "Eternal Darkness" continues to expand.

item.title: NRL referee Ashley Klein confirms he had a gambling problem after report claims he lost $400,000
item.description: Origin referee Ashley Klein has confirmed he battled with a gambling addiction after a report claimed he lost $400,000.

item.title: Socceroos Mo Touré and Tete Yengi to realise World Cup dreams
item.description: The Touré brothers snuck into their local football field after hours to play the game they loved, while the Yengi brothers kicked around makeshift balls made of plastic bags. Two of them have now made it onto the world stage as Socceroos.

item.title: SANFL players consider industrial action as hourly salary drops to a third of minimum wage
item.description: The SANFL Players' Association is discussing the potential of a strike in response to a 50 per cent decline in pay over the past 20 years.

item.title: Safe Havens pop-up mental health clinics close across Northern Rivers
item.description: Set up in the wake of 2022's catastrophic floods, these drop-in mental health centres in the Northern Rivers are shutting down.

item.title: Donald Trump says a ceasefire deal is 'close', but it could be on Iran's terms
item.description: Amid rising expectations a pathway to peace between Iran and the US is close, the reported details of a ceasefire deal appear to show Iran getting numerous concessions from the White House.

item.title: Investors weigh pros and cons of boarding Elon Musk's $2 trillion SpaceX 'rocket'
item.description: SpaceX operates rockets, spaceflight, telecommunications, and AI — but Elon Musk now wants to establish a self-sustaining city on Mars, with at least 1 million inhabitants.

item.title: Closure of city's only gay club widened Hobart drag kings' audience
item.description: Hobart's drag kings say the closure of a gay bar pushed their performances into heteronormative spaces and a surprisingly accepting Tasmanian community.

item.title: Taylor Swift, Alanis Morissette among Songwriters Hall of Fame 2026 inductees
item.description: Taylor Swift has become the youngest woman to be inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame. She's among the writers behind some of pop's biggest hits to be inducted in 2026.

item.title: Virat Kohli pays tribute to Kane Williamson as New Zealand legend retires from cricket
item.description: New Zealand cricketing legend Kane Williamson announces his retirement from the game immediately after a stunning career.

item.title: Family creates bespoke 'university of life' campus for daughter Lillian, an NDIS participant
item.description: When Lillian Rowsell was in her final year of school and unable to continue formal education, her parents found a creative solution.

item.title: Former Liberal strategist, polling company owner Brad Stansfield in Senate tilt
item.description: A former Liberal adviser turned Tasmanian political commentator says he is "genuinely worried about the future of the country" and plans to stand for preselection for the Senate at the next federal election.

item.title: Charity Youth Beat takes to Perth's streets to help young people in need
item.description: Youth Beat workers take to Perth streets at night to help young people who have nowhere else to go.

item.title: Australia has valuable cards to play in the AI future, but critical gaps remain, new report finds
item.description: A new report says Australia can capitalise on AI, with valuable cards to play strategically.

item.title: Public swimming pools an oasis in remote communities
item.description: In Kalumburu and Balgo, two of Western Australia's most isolated towns, investing in a community swimming pool has been a boon for everyone.

item.title: Trump says US has killed leader of Venezuelan drug cartel Tren de Aragua in air strike
item.description: The US president said he would continue to track down Tren de Aragua members and "send them to the depths of hell where they belong".

item.title: Workers remove Donald Trump's name from Kennedy Center after court denies last-minute appeal
item.description: Workers are removing the US president's name from the facade of the building hours after a court-ordered deadline.

item.title: Casey McLean ruled out of State of Origin II for Blues, Mark Nawaqanitawase to make debut
item.description: Casey McLean is ruled out of Origin II at the MCG after going down with a quad injury at training on the Central Coast.

item.title: US launches strike on leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Rewards club' lottery billionaire Adrian Portelli sues former SA minister
item.description: Melbourne "rewards club" lottery billionaire Adrian Portelli has launched a defamation lawsuit against former SA minister Andrea Michaels over comments she made about a $3.5 million home prize draw.

item.title: Use up the veggies in your fridge with this comforting Sri Lankan soup - ABC News
item.description: This soup recipe makes the most of bone-in beef offcuts and whatever veggies are cheap to make a humble but rich meal.

item.title: Australians lose access to 'dangerous' Anthropic AI models after Trump order
item.description: The US government restrictions cite national security concerns over the AI models Fable 5 and Mythos 5.

item.title: Swans steal thriller with spirited second-half comeback against Port
item.description: Sydney's big guns all stand up tall in the final quarter to help secure a three-point win over Port Adelaide in Adelaide as North Melbourne escapes against West Coast.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Legends stunned by on-air gaffe, Donald Trump's call goes cold
item.description: The US and Canada hit the pitch for the first time at their home World Cup, but on day two the drama extended from the field to the TV booths and beyond. Here are the quick hits.

item.title: US Justice Department green lights Paramount's acquisition of Warner Bros
item.description: The Trump administration has given the green light for Paramount Skydance to take over Warner Bros Discovery in a $US110 billion ($156 billion) mega-merger.

item.title: Body of young girl recovered from Sydney bay where father was found dead
item.description: Police divers have recovered the body of a young girl from a bay in Sydney's inner west.

item.title: Rainforest conservationist Tony Parkes dies aged 96 after a career of 'world-leading science'
item.description: After falling in love with Australia's ancient Big Scrub rainforest, Tony Parkes built his life around saving its final remnants and restoring hundreds of hectares.

item.title: World Cup: Tony Popovic returns to the underdog well, as Türkiye dismiss Socceroos
item.description: Australia's upcoming opponents, Türkiye, have generational talent and are keen to end generational heartache.

item.title: Man faces murder charge after woman's body found near Kalgoorlie-Boulder highway
item.description: Bevan Watson, 38, faced the Kalgoorlie Magistrates Court on Monday, charged with murdering the 35-year-old woman whose body was found on the side of Goldfields Highway.

item.title: Raiders hit rock bottom in shocking loss to Eels
item.description: Canberra's season has fallen apart with last year's minor premiers slumping to yet another loss following a spirited Parramatta performance.

item.title: Police say friend raised alarm before body found in Bayview Park, Sydney
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: US and Iran edge closer to signing peace deal, though Tehran disputes Sunday timeline
item.description: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif says a deal aimed at ending the war in the Middle East is closer than "ever before" and is expected to be finalised within 24 hours.

item.title: Scotland claims first World Cup win in 36 years as Brazil, Morocco share spoils
item.description: Brazil and Morocco share the spoils in a blockbuster clash after Qatar salvaged a late draw against Switzerland in San Francisco, while Scotland hung on to beat Haiti.

item.title: Can you soar to the top of Guy Montgomery's Spelling Bee quiz — or will you be a sore loser?
item.description: From homophones to Aussie bird names, Guy Montgomery's Spelling Bee quiz is set to test and teach in equal measure.

item.title: One goal, many journeys
item.description: The Socceroos squad is like no other that's ever been sent to represent us at a World Cup. See the extraordinary journeys that forged a team to represent modern Australia on the world stage.

item.title: Some people believe Britain is 'broken', and they are looking to Australia for populist inspiration
item.description: Right-wing populism is rising in the UK where, like Australia, the political landscape is looking very different lately. It has prompted some people to ask if these surging parties can deliver on their promises.

item.title: As the US marks freedom from a king, Donald Trump makes Washington DC his own
item.description: Project by project, Donald Trump is altering the United States capital, and in some cases, disrupting views and design that date back more than two centuries.

item.title: Here's what to know if you're jumping on the Socceroos World Cup bandwagon
item.description: The world's biggest sporting event is back on our screens, and Australia is just hours away from taking the pitch. Here's a quick guide to get you across the basics.

item.title: Australia's invisible mothers
item.description: For the Pacific Islander women who harvest the fruit and vegetables that stock Australia’s supermarket shelves, motherhood here can mean injury, debt, secrecy and fear.

item.title: From wine to water, Adelaide Hills charity OpBlue brings hope to children in Kenya
item.description: From the Adelaide Hills to refugee camps in Kenya, how one young Aussie has made it his mission to help.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: When do the Socceroos play and how to watch Australia vs Türkiye
item.description: The Socceroos will face Türkiye in their first Group D match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Here's everything you need to know.

item.title: Families of seafarers killed in Strait of Hormuz demand to know who is accountable
item.description: Families of Indian sailors who died in a US military attack in the Strait of Hormuz want to know who is accountable for attacks on commercial vessels.

item.title: KPMG to face day of reckoning over audit scandal, 30 witnesses to appear at hearing
item.description: Accounting firm KPMG is engulfed in a scandal that has seen several senior leaders quit and ASIC launch an investigation into the firm's business dealings.

item.title: Older Australians with motor neurone disease consider early euthanasia under aged care system
item.description: The death of AFL legend Neale Daniher has shone a light on the grim choices faced by older Australians fighting motor neurone disease.

item.title: Australia defeats South Africa in opening clash of women's T20 World Cup
item.description: Australia puts its rivals on notice with a dominant victory over South Africa to kick-off the women's T20 World Cup.

item.title: Tinariwen on war, exile and the power of Tuareg music
item.description: Legendary African band Tinariwen say there’s very little hope for their country. It just makes their dedication to music more important.

item.title: The Nununurra shipbuilding yard
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Shoalhaven's Orient Point Estate streets named after luxury steam ocean liners
item.description: Orient Point, where the streets are named after ships beginning with "O", shows how national pride in 19th and early 20th-century boatbuilding featured in colonial town planning.

item.title: Nine-year-old Perth girl fatally shot by police in Pakistan while on family holiday
item.description: A nine-year-old Perth girl has been fatally shot by police in Pakistan after officers mistook her family's car for that of suspected criminals.

item.title: Art Gallery of NSW Super Art Heroes program uses storytelling to engage young creative minds
item.description: This group of pint-sized superheroes is taking part in an early childhood education tour run by the Art Gallery of NSW, which teaches young minds about shapes, colour and form.

item.title: Peruvian police dress as World Cup mascots to arrest suspected drug dealer
item.description: Police in Peru dress up as World Cup mascots to arrest a suspected drug dealer who was caught up in football fever.

item.title: Adelaide Thunderbirds break Super Netball record with 13-win season
item.description: This year's record-breaking Adelaide Thunderbirds are shifting their focus to the Super Netball finals, with a third premiership in four years on offer.

item.title: Liberal senator Jonno Duniam makes 'tough call' to leave politics before the end of this year
item.description: Senator Duniam says it is time to prioritise his family after 25 years in politics, and he will leave his position in the Senate by the end of this year.

item.title: The ACT requires all packaging at public events be recyclable or compostable but most of it ends up in landfill
item.description: The ACT mandates reusable, recyclable and compostable packaging at all public events, but nearly all of it ends up in landfill because there is no facility to compost it.

item.title: Storytelling, sport, theatre and culture — this remote school camp is one of a kind
item.description: Children from across Central Australia have joined a remote Northern Territory camp inside the Watarrka National Park where "it's cool to go to school" — and the footy doesn't hurt either.

item.title: The season doesn't matter as wildflower hunters can always find gold in WA
item.description: WA has a reputation for putting on incredible wildflower displays, with those on the ground saying you can find something special all year round.

item.title: UK celebrates King Charles's official birthday with Trooping the Colour
item.description: Members of the royal family attend the king's annual birthday parade in London.

item.title: Former WA potato farm transformed into wetland sanctuary for rare and endangered birds
item.description: Once drained for farm irrigation, this former potato farm now attracts birds from as far away as Siberia.

item.title: Maroons rally around Tino Fa'asuamaleaui after father's death ahead of Origin II
item.description: Tino Fa'asuamaleaui indicates he plans to play in Origin II after the death of his father this week.

item.title: Mark Butler hits back after 'extraordinary submission' against NDIS changes
item.description: Health Minister Mark Butler is open to adjusting the proposed National Disability Insurance Scheme changes, but insists the government is on the right track.

item.title: Thousands rally in Belfast to condemn anti-immigrant rioting that followed stabbing
item.description: The anti-racism rally came after nights of fiery violence in parts of Northern Ireland following the arrest of a 30-year-old man from Sudan on charges of attempted murder in a stabbing that left a man partly blind.

item.title: Hundreds of regional firefighters rally against controversial levy in Bendigo
item.description: Hundreds of regional Victorian firefighters turn out for a rally in Bendigo, calling on the state government to scrap a controversial levy after saying they have been "left behind".

item.title: Trafficking survivors still living on the streets months after Cambodian scam compound exodus
item.description: Hundreds of trafficking survivors from Africa are left stranded in Cambodia amid a widespread crackdown on the country's scam compounds.

item.title: Socceroos ecstasy as wondergoals deliver 2-0 World Cup victory over Türkiye
item.description: The Socceroos have claimed an incredible 2-0 win over Türkiye in their first World Cup group match thanks to sensational goals from Nestory Irankunda and Connor Metcalfe.

item.title: Brisbane City Council to invest more than $5 million in health and wellness activities in Wednesday's budget
item.description: The Move Well Brisbane campaign will double the number of providers and activities in Brisbane's Active and Healthy program and include nutritional and cooking classes.

item.title: Love story: From childhood sweethearts to soulmates
item.description: It was a case of puppy love when Cambell first set eyes on Tiana at a Gold Coast housing complex when they were both nine years old. They lost touch when they entered their teens but reunited as adults and have been inseparable since.

item.title: Ryan kicks six as Saints get home in GWS thriller at Docklands
item.description: Liam Ryan kicks half a dozen goals as St Kilda earns a thrilling eight-point win over Greater Western Sydney, after Brisbane's victory in Hobart over the Tigers.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: England camp raided by thieves as Scotland celebrations erupt
item.description: England's team bus is raided by thieves while victorious Scottish fans prepare for a rowdy long weekend of celebrations. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: West Antarctic winter ice fails to form in Bellingshausen Sea in area 'size of France'
item.description: Scientists say a chunk of winter sea ice the size of France off the Antarctic coast has failed to form this year.

item.title: Jet skis, drones monitor Coogee Beach after shark attack
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Drones patrol Coogee Beach after ban lifted as NSW government won't rule out shark culls
item.description: The renewed focus on NSW's shark program comes a day after a woman swimming between the flags, close to shore, was bitten at Coogee Beach.

item.title: Independent watchdog to probe complaints against WA judges under new legislation
item.description: Complaints about the behaviour and capacity of judges and magistrates will be investigated by an independent watchdog, under new legislation to be introduced to WA parliament this week.

item.title: Luai leads Tigers to epic final Leichhardt win
item.description: Jarome Luai has lead the Wests Tigers to a win in their final match at Leichhardt Oval before it's redevelopment.

item.title: World Cup Socceroos quick hits: Nestory Irankunda's goal and Patrick Beach's save in win over Türkiye
item.description: Nestory Irankunda scored a goal for the ages and then celebrated like Tim Cahill, while Patrick Beach's palm proved enormous. Here are the five quick hits from the Socceroos vs Türkiye.

item.title: World Cup: Socceroos' great gamble pays off as Patrick Beach vindicates Tony Popovic's big call
item.description: With his first World Cup team selection, Tony Popovic produced one of the biggest shocks in Socceroos' history. After 90 minutes against Türkiye, his gamble was vindicated.

item.title: Tasmanian Labor, Greens call for Racing Minister Jane Howlett to resign over unanswered legal bill questions
item.description: The Tasmanian Greens have joined Labor's calls for Racing Minister Jane Howlett to resign over a failure to answer questions about her publicly funded legal proceedings.

item.title: Jubilant fans pack Federation Square to celebrate Socceroos win over Türkiye
item.description: In Melbourne, Federation Square became the epicentre of fan festivities, with capacity crowds packing into the CBD venue for a Socceroos viewing party.

item.title: Melbourne fans overjoyed as Socceroos scored against Türkiye in World Cup
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Socceroos boast heavy Adelaide connection — here's how SA turned out to support them
item.description: Thousands of South Australian soccer fans descended on The Drive in Adelaide city to watch Adelaide's own Nestory Irankunda score the first half-opener, leading to Australia's win over Türkiye on Sunday.

item.title: Why experts are tipping interest rates to pause
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: British forces intercept Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker in the English Channel
item.description: The British armed forces intercept a sanctioned oil tanker in a bid to disrupt the oil revenue ​that helps fund Russia's war in Ukraine.

item.title: Sweden's Premier League stars shine to cast Tunisia aside
item.description: Sweden dispatches Tunisia after Côte d'Ivoire scores a last-minute winner against Ecuador and Japan finds a late leveller against the Netherlands.

item.title: Race Around The World recap: From Jamaica to Greenland, catch the week two films
item.description: From dating in Jamaica to world domination in Greenland, catch up on the films from the second week of the new season of Race Around The World.

item.title: This week in history quiz, June 15: Test yourself on these past events
item.description: When was Salt Lake City awarded the XIX Winter Olympics? And how much do you know about the Fast and the Furious films? Test yourself in this week's history quiz.

item.title: What to watch in June, from homegrown romance to remote mystery
item.description: Plus Brisbane is a main character in a new rom-com, Russell T Davies delivers a devastating drama, and we bring you more shows lighting up TV this month.

item.title: Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days just weeks before he died
item.description: Just 11 weeks before he died, Richard Scolyer cycled 500km in four days. In his last major TV interview, the celebrated pathologist tells Australian Story the secret to a fulfilling life and his doctor reveals the huge risks he took to try new brain cancer treatment.

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX trillions are fuelled by AI and his pied piper power over investors
item.description: To get a sense of the difference between the Tesla and SpaceX IPOs, consider that 1.7 billion seconds ago was 1972, when Elton John released Rocket Man, while 1.7 trillion seconds ago was 52,000BC, when we shared the planet with Neanderthals.

item.title: Prolonged pause on interest rates a possibility as RBA board meets for June
item.description: Most experts are tipping interest rates will remain on hold but the question is when will they come down.

item.title: Dashdot encouraged customers to proceed days before collapse as liquidators examine insolvency timeline
item.description: Emails and messages seen by ABC News show Dashdot's staff were still encouraging prospective customers to engage with the buyers agency shortly before it entered voluntary liquidation.

item.title: Palestinians fear 'archaeology has become a tool' in Israel's plans in the West Bank
item.description: Palestinians believe there is more to Israel's recently announced plans to develop heritage sites along the West Bank, arguing archaeology has "become a tool, a mechanism, to justify settlement and justify the displacement of Palestinians" across the area.

item.title: Call to reinstate travel compensation scheme after millions lost in AVG Travels collapse
item.description: Australian travellers who collectively lost millions after a travel agency collapsed are calling for an overhaul of consumer protections in the sector.

item.title: Multi-billion-dollar economic policy undermined by 'black cladding'
item.description: Almost $15 billion has been spent over a decade via the federal government's Indigenous Procurement Policy, but critics say it's failed to make a significant difference to Indigenous people due to the practice of "black cladding".

item.title: Locals affected by Ireland's AI data centre boom share 'cautionary tales'
item.description: As Australia and other countries embark on a new wave of AI-driven data centre development, locals in Ireland share how the runaway growth of the facilities can affect everyday life.

item.title: Business groups call on parliament to 'reject' CGT changes as inquiry kicks off
item.description: Business groups are urging the government to scrap its capital gains tax changes as the first tranche of measures is scrutinised at a parliamentary inquiry.

item.title: Japan to roll out nationwide LGBTQIA+ education for the first time
item.description: The country is taking a big step in its efforts to address gay rights but not everyone is convinced it goes far enough.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Cowboy country turns into football epicentre in Dallas, Texas
item.description: Japan and Netherlands fans are turning out in big numbers in Dallas, Texas, as the city embraces its status as the main World Cup hub.

item.title: AI company Firmus to become Tasmania's biggest power user if three planned sites come to fruition
item.description: Artificial intelligence company Firmus Technologies will become Tasmania's biggest power user if its ambitions to establish three "AI factories" in the state come to fruition.

item.title: Swiss voters set to reject population cap in referendum
item.description: A projection shows voters prioritising economic stability and the country's ties with the European Union over immigration concerns.

item.title: World Cup: Socceroo Nestory Irankunda knew how he would celebrate before even taking the field
item.description: Nestory Irankunda's childhood hero is none other than Tim Cahill. So it was fitting that more than 20 years after Cahill's famous celebration, this generation's Socceroos hero paid tribute.

item.title: Concerns over Great Artesian Basin water impacts in new BHP agreement
item.description: Environmentalists and First Nations groups say a new mining agreement with BHP does not do enough to end water extraction from the Great Artesian Basin. But the SA government says the agreement imposes stricter environmental regulation.

item.title: World Cup: Pat Beach's Socceroos dream is Mat Ryan's nightmare, and Mark Schwarzer can relate to both
item.description: Pat Beach became a surprise national hero with his performance against Türkiye, while incumbent goalkeeper Mat Ryan watched from the bench. Mark Schwarzer has experienced both sides of that coin.

item.title: Iran weekly briefing: In the build-up to the ceasefire, Trump resumed dropping f-bombs
item.description: Donald Trump is reported to unleash another expletive-laden outburst at Benjamin Netanyahu over Israeli strikes as the US and Iran finalise a peace deal.

item.title: PFAS queries continue as Wagga residents reassured drinking water is safe
item.description: Wagga Wagga has two known sites contaminated by "forever chemicals" and some say not enough information about how it is being managed is available.

item.title: Gold Coast nurse charged over alleged offences involving six teenage girls
item.description: Detectives charged the 58-year-old man following investigations into the alleged indecent treatment of children between November 2020 and May 2024.

item.title: Cooper Connolly hits stunning century to hand Australia victory over Bangladesh in third ODI
item.description: Cooper Connolly fights off his body going into shock due to cramps to record his maiden international century.

item.title: Gina Rinehart makes 'significant investment' in SpaceX — as it happened
item.description: The local share market and Australian dollar rose after Donald Trump confirmed a ceasefire agreement had been reached with Iran. Hancock Prospecting spruiks its stake in Elon Musk's mega IPO.

item.title: Northern Grampians Shire Council ignores community calls for CCTV at Stawell crime 'hotspot'
item.description: Parents are working to petition a west Victorian council to install CCTV cameras at a skate park that police are calling a crime "hotspot". The council says taking on a public safety role is "unreasonable".

item.title: Property Council hits back at claim tax debate was 'submerged by vested interests' — as it happened
item.description: Housing groups have addressed the Senate inquiry into the federal government’s sweeping changes to negative gearing and capital gains.

item.title: 'Potential explosive hazards' at Shackleton and Scott's historically important huts in Antarctica
item.description: Chemicals found inside two of Antarctica's most important historical sites, Earnest Shackleton's and Robert Falcon Scott's huts, could pose "potential explosive hazards", a report has warns.

item.title: A health worker holding an inject that cures syphilis
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: US and Iran announce ceasefire and plans to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States and Iran reach an agreement to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz, offering relief to the global economy after three months of fighting.

item.title: World Cup: Socceroos hero Nestory Irankunda hits back as Türkiye star says his team 'dominated'
item.description: Socceroos goalscoring hero Nestory Irankunda says Türkiye could not back up its talk, despite one of its stars claiming post-game Australia had been "dominated".

item.title: Alex de Minaur claims runner-up in Rosmalen Championships after tie-break loss in final
item.description: Australia's top-ranked men's player Alex de Minaur fights to a tie-break in the Rosmalen Championships final but falls to his Polish opponent.

item.title: Plans to open Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran ceasefire reached — as it happened
item.description: Pakistan, which has been negotiating peace talks between the US and Iran, says a deal to end the war has been agreed.

item.title: Australians are dying from syphilis, a disease that was almost eliminated more than a decade ago
item.description: Forty-two babies in Australia died from congenital syphilis between 2011 and 2025, with the outbreak driving health workers into communities to raise awareness of the old-world disease.

item.title: WA needle exchanges raise concerns over 'dramatic' rise in injectable peptide use
item.description: Needle exchanges in Western Australia are reporting dramatic increases in people accessing their services for peptide use.

item.title: A lost Sam Pang went looking for something. He found comedy
item.description: This workplace satire starring Sam Pang has Tasmania's first ever AFL team firmly in its sights.

item.title: Picturesque islands in WA's north set to become transport hubs after $20m sale
item.description: A Kimberley iron ore mine with idyllic views across a tropical sea will close after 70 years of operation as a new phase begins for offshore oil and gas.

item.title: White House UFC event ends with massive upset win in title fight
item.description: US President Donald Trump watches ring-side as the UFC event dubbed Freedom 250 ends with Justin Gaethje scoring a massive upset win over Ilia Topuria at the White House.

item.title: Police treating deaths of man and girl in bay at Concord as murder-suicide
item.description: Investigators are trying to determine what led to a suspected murder-suicide on Saturday, after the bodies of a father and young child were recovered from a bay off Concord.

item.title: China's ability to hit Australia with missiles 'real and growing', Lowy Institute says
item.description: New analysis warns that China's military is developing a "real and growing" ability to hit the Australian mainland with missiles, and can already threaten Australia's trade routes, subsea cables and critical infrastructure.

item.title: Musician and online personality Oliver Tree presumed dead after helicopter crash
item.description: Ahead of his Australian tour, the American musician and online personality is reportedly one of six passengers involved in a fatal helicopter collision.

item.title: World Cup: FIFA's hydration break farce the ultimate insult for weary football fans
item.description: Football fans are forced to endure much to keep their love of the beautiful game alive, but FIFA's cynical new "hydration breaks" are stretching the friendship like never before.

item.title: Before Donald Trump's Iran deal, he and Benjamin Netanyahu were pursuing separate agendas
item.description: As Donald Trump was trying to push his Iran deal over the line, Benjamin Netanyahu bombed Beirut, showing once again the Israeli prime minister has other priorities.

item.title: Bureau of Meteorology's only SA wave buoy offline for third time in two years
item.description: The wave buoy off Kangaroo Island is the only such device operated by the Bureau of Meteorology in South Australia and is relied upon by professional divers and fishers.

item.title: Magnitude-2.9 earthquake felt near large dam in NSW Snowy Mountains
item.description: The magnitude-2.9 earthquake was felt in parts of the Snowy Mountains just before 1pm on Sunday, according to Geosceince Australia.

item.title: Perth girl Hania Ahmed remembered after fatal police shooting in Pakistan
item.description: A nine-year-old girl fatally shot by police who were pursuing armed robbers in Pakistan is remembered as happy and bubbly by the principal of her Perth school, as her family tries to deal with their "tragic loss".

item.title: Liberty Bell Bay smelter given another reprieve, with workers keeping their jobs for now
item.description: The administrator for the mothballed manganese smelter is continuing to engage with the consortium designated as its preferred buyer, the ABC understands.

item.title: Tasmanian man allegedly caught drink driving three times in just over a week
item.description: A Tasmanian man has been charged after being allegedly caught drink driving for the third time in just over a week, with him having to be cut from his car in one instance.

item.title: Canberra court hears paramedic 'haunted' by woman's death at hands of partner has since quit job
item.description: Those affected by the murder of Tiffany Spence, who died from horrific injuries inflicted by her partner Adam Britt, tell a sentencing hearing of the long-term impacts of her death.

item.title: Ipswich man Bobby Weaver sentenced to life in jail after murdering neighbour and hiding remains in freezer
item.description: Justice Paul Smith says Bobby Weaver murdered his friend, 58-year-old David Thornton, "in cold blood".

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Curaçao's special goal and De Oranje brighten up day four
item.description: De Oranje hit the World Cup and in the middle of a 7-1 thumping, Curaçao's lone goal might have meant the most of all. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Why Cameron Munster is always Queensland's State of Origin saviour
item.description: Ever since his first State of Origin match, Queensland has looked to Cameron Munster in times of trouble. Now, with the series on the line at the MCG, his state will ask its greatest modern hero to do so again.

item.title: Lebanon and nuclear weapons: The stumbling blocks in Trump's Iran negotiation
item.description: The US-Iran deal faces five volatile days as questions remain over nuclear weapons and Israel's attacks on Lebanon.

item.title: 'Fakeaway' recipes to feed the family
item.description: Ordering in of an evening can become a costly exercise but these five tasty recipes are sure to hit the spot.

item.title: Police investigate dozens of dirt bike riders in Newcastle after Bad Apples 'riot' in Dangar Park
item.description: Newcastle residents and authorities condemn a clothing company stunt that degenerated into a "riot" involving dozens of dirt bike riders.

item.title: South Australian community, family reacts to Nestory Irankunda's World Cup goal
item.description: The family and community of Socceroos star Nestory Irankunda are "very emotional" after his goal-scoring World Cup debut. His cousin says he will be "forever grateful" for the support the family received after arriving in Australia, and "their blessing is seeing this at the big stage".

item.title: Australian swimming great Kaylee McKeown weighing up Olympic future beyond 2028 Los Angeles Games
item.description: As she prepares for the Glasgow Commonwealth Games, Kaylee McKeown will not rule out extending her career through to the 2032 Brisbane Olympics.

item.title: Victoria Police investigate off-field brawl during Ararat and Lismore-Derrinallum football match
item.description: Victoria Police is investigating an off-field fight at a local football match in Ararat over the weekend, sparking concerns about "dreadful" behaviour at grassroots sporting events.

item.title: Former AFL footballer Nick Stevens jailed over $170k Mildura pool fraud
item.description: An ex-footballer has been locked up after a jury found him guilty of defrauding multiple families with his regional pool business.

item.title: NT government introduces new air quality testing plan, including measuring carcinogenic emissions
item.description: The Northern Territory government has announced a plan to introduce new air quality monitoring of toxic pollutants, including any carcinogenic emissions. It follows a review that found gas giant Inpex had underestimated its emissions in Darwin.

item.title: Police charge two men after improvised gun discoveries in SA
item.description: South Australian police have separately arrested two men for the alleged possession of a handgun disguised as a mobile phone, and a homemade "pen gun".

item.title: Fuel excise rebate call next week as Anthony Albanese says ceasefire does not mean 'return to normal'
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says the government will give Australians "appropriate notice" about the fuel excise rebate, as it continues to monitor the issue.

item.title: Sydney mother mauled by shark at Coogee Beach has undergone 'multiple surgeries', family says
item.description: Leah Stewart was swimming at Coogee Beach when she was attacked just before 11am on Saturday.

item.title: Gold Coast nurse charged over alleged stalking, abuse of teenage girls
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Missing dog rescued from Glenelg breakwater
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dog rescued after five-day ordeal trapped underneath Glenelg breakwater
item.description: A 14-year-old fox terrier is back in her owner's arms after a multi-day stint trapped in the Glenelg breakwater after she broke free from her leash.

item.title: Drug harm advocates warn the Canberra Liberals' proposal to recriminalise methamphetamine ignores evidence
item.description: Drug harm reduction advocates says there is little evidence that methamphetamine is more harmful in the ACT since being decriminalised, despite a Canberra Liberals commitment to recriminalise it if elected.

item.title: Gold Coast average general rates up 4.7pc, debt to rise beyond $1b as budget adopted
item.description: Councillors have adopted the $2.7 billion budget, which is focused on major projects and frontline services.

item.title: World War II Lancaster bomber pilot Howard Hendrick dies aged 102
item.description: Howard Hendrick's life was dedicated to service in the Royal Australian Air Force and to his community. His contributions will forever remembered by his family and his hometown.

item.title: Stepson of Norway's crown prince convicted of rape, sentenced to 4 years' jail
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, 29, has been found guilty of two counts of rape and other crimes.

item.title: Economists defend capital gains changes despite flaws as business groups line up in opposition
item.description: Economists have offered qualified support for the government's plans on day one of a Senate inquiry.

item.title: Four-week coronial inquest into Kumanjayi White's death in police custody set for April 2027
item.description: A four-week coronial inquest into the death of a 24-year-old man who died in a Coles supermarket in Alice Springs last year after being restrained by police officers has been set down for April 2027.

item.title: Man accused of lighting Mount Gambier Valley Lake bushfire has charge dismissed
item.description: A man who was charged after a fire near Mount Gambier will still have to appear in court to face harm, theft and offensive language charges.

item.title: Coroner finds Josh Warneke was murdered in Broome, identifies serious WA Police failings
item.description: A coroner has found the investigation into a Broome tradies' murder, which remains an enduring mystery, was mishandled by WA Police.

item.title: Firefighters battle flames after cathedral hit by Russian strikes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Cairns man charged after allegedly naming prominent Queenslander in extortion case
item.description: A Cairns man accused of exposing the identity of a Queensland public figure at the centre of a mysterious extortion case has been charged by police with allegedly disobeying a suppression order.

item.title: Jamie Lawrence Powrie charged over $5.7 million bitcoin bust released on bail
item.description: Prosecutors allege the house Jamie Lawrence Powrie was arrested at was bought with the cryptocurrency acquired through illegal dark web activity.

item.title: Raelene Polymiadis stands trial for allegedly murdering parents with fatal doses of insulin
item.description: Adelaide woman Raelene Polymiadis is standing trial for two counts of murder after allegedly deliberately administering "fatal doses of insulin" to her parents in separate incidents, just over a year apart.

item.title: UK prime minister says he will ban social media for under-16s
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer says he will ban social media sites for under-16s and impose restrictions on gaming and live streaming platforms in some of the most far-reaching online restrictions globally.

item.title: Pressure mounts on Tasmanian Liberal Jane Howlett over legal bill and election messaging
item.description: Tasmania's racing minister is facing mounting calls to resign from cabinet, after evidence in parliament called into question what her office knew about messaging that breached election guidelines.

item.title: Bold BBL plan within reach after Cricket Australia meets with states
item.description: A meeting between Cricket Australia and the six state bodies has ended in significant steps being taken towards BBL privatisation.

item.title: Victorian MPs take plunge for Neale Daniher's FightMND Big Freeze fundraiser
item.description: State government and opposition MPs take the plunge to raise tens of thousands of dollars for motor neurone disease research just days after Neale Daniher's state funeral.

item.title: Alleged people smuggler paid $20k to sail to Indonesia with fugitive Queensland bikie
item.description: Queensland woman Jessica Beckett told a West Australian court she did not know an outlaw bikie gang member was on the boat she was sailing to Indonesia.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: As progress dips in men's netball, the Giants may have played their last game
item.description: Despite men's netballers making a valuable contribution to the Aussie Diamonds pathway and Super Netball competition, progress between the two genders at the international level has largely been scaled back and unexplained.

item.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Prosecutors allege Brisbane teen planned attack on the Liberal Party
item.description: Prosecutor says a private schoolboy who allegedly planned to bomb a public event was inspired by the "Unabomber".

item.title: Four killed in Kyiv as UNESCO-listed monastery struck by Russian missile
item.description: Four people have been killed after a Russian strike hit a 900-year-old monastery in central Kyiv.

item.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz about to open?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: The family behind Socceroo hero Nestory Irankunda
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: AFP inquiring into flotilla activist claims of sexual assault by Israeli defence personnel
item.description: Australian Federal Police have begun inquiries into allegations that Israeli defence personnel sexually assaulted Australian activists on a flotilla headed to Gaza, following a meeting with Foreign Minister Penny Wong.

item.title: Two men convicted of plot to torch houses and a car linked to British PM Keir Starmer
item.description: A Russian-speaking figure who went by the name "El Money" offered one of the men money to torch the properties and take video that could be posted online to draw attention to the attack.

item.title: Seven Australians are dying each day from drug overdose in worst year on record, researchers say
item.description: Australia recorded its deadliest year on record for drug overdoses, according to a new analysis, with an average of seven deaths per day.

item.title: 1,000 people killed by drones this year in Sudan as humanitarian crisis grows
item.description: The United Nations has condemned an increase in drone attacks in conflict-torn Sudan.

item.title: Live: Cabo Verde denies Spain in one of World Cup's biggest-ever upsets
item.description: World Cup debutant Cabo Verde produce an incredible 90-minute performance to hold favourites Spain goalless, taking a point in Atlanta. Follow live.

item.title: Mixed messages surround US-Iran deal as Lebanon emerges as stumbling block
item.description: Conflicting information is being released about the US-Iran ceasefire agreement announced at the weekend, including whether Iran will introduce tolls on the Strait of Hormuz in the future.

item.title: Flag quiz, part 3: Hoist the colours and try to answer these questions
item.description: Which country's flag is the only one with a modern weapon on it? And which US state has the Union Jack on its flag?

item.title: James Bond: 007 First Light is the secret agent's best outing in decades
item.description: While producers are still mulling over who will take the Bond mantle from Daniel Craig, the super-spy has already moved on in 007 First Light.

item.title: Cooling economy and Iran backdown to keep RBA at bay
item.description: Reserve Bank board members will be feeling relieved today when they meet to thrash out our future interest rate movements.

item.title: Interest rates and tax shake-up see buyer demand weaken in property market
item.description: Interest rate hikes, tax changes and global economic uncertainty force first home buyers and investors to press pause on property purchases.

item.title: In a season of tight finishes, these are the AFL teams coming up clutch in the final moments
item.description: It's been a season of tight finishes in the AFL, but which teams are holding their nerve the best and why?

item.title: Transgrid seeks $1.1b from consumers in blowout 'litmus test' for energy transition
item.description: Transgrid wants to claw back huge cost increases for a major transmission line from consumers. Experts say the regulator's decision will have far-reaching consequences.

item.title: Saving might feel impossible, but there are five reasons to keep trying
item.description: Saving is not the only tool you need to achieve financial security these days, but it still has a role to play if you can manage it.

item.title: How Captain Cook went from being celebrated to slain in Hawaii
item.description: Captain Cook set sail from Plymouth to find an elusive trade route linking Europe to Asia in 1776. He never saw England again.

item.title: Tasmanian senator Jonno Duniam's resignation could open door for One Nation
item.description: Tasmanian senator Jonno Duniam's announcement that he intends to leave politics could be the leg up that One Nation needs.
